message:
fix(pager): restore RESERVED lock level after group commit flush failure

Previously, if the WAL append_frames or sync call failed during group
commit, the code would skip the unlock(RESERVED) downgrade, potentially
leaving the pager stuck in an EXCLUSIVE lock state and blocking all
other readers/writers.

Fix by wrapping the flush I/O logic (append + sync + db_size update) in
an inner closure and running unlock(cx, LockLevel::Reserved)
unconditionally afterward, regardless of flush success or failure.
If both the flush and the unlock fail, a combined error message is
produced.

Add test_group_commit_flush_failure_restores_reserved_lock_level using
a FailingGroupCommitWalBackend to verify the lock is properly downgraded
to RESERVED after flush failure and fully released on drop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
